declarations.name: initials
declarations.description:
Initials from a display name, for the avatar fallback. Exported for tests + as
part of the public API. Delegates to the shared internal `initialsFromName` (the
one reviewed implementation, also used by <jeswr-login-panel>'s `initialsOf`).

The top-right account control: avatar + display name, opening a dropdown with
the WebID and a Sign out action. Decoupled via attributes; presentation chrome.
No RDF data model (the host supplies `webid`/`name`/`avatar-url`).
declarations.name: JeswrAccountMenu

cssParts.description: The avatar+name button that opens the menu.
cssParts.name: trigger

cssParts.description: The name shown next to the avatar in the trigger.
cssParts.name: trigger-name

cssParts.description: The name+WebID block at the top of the menu.
cssParts.name: identity

cssParts.description: The WebID line in the identity block.
cssParts.name: webid

slots.description: Extra menu items injected above "Sign out".

members.name: webId
members.description: The authenticated user's WebID (shown under the name; the canonical id).

members.name: name
members.description: Human display name (foaf:name). Falls back to the WebID, then "Account".

members.name: avatarUrl
members.description: Avatar image URL (foaf:img / vcard:hasPhoto). Falls back to initials.

events.description: The user activated "Sign out" (no detail); host tears down the session.
events.name: sign-out

attributes.name: webid
attributes.description: The authenticated user's WebID (shown under the name; the canonical id).

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Human display name (foaf:name). Falls back to the WebID, then "Account".

attributes.name: avatar-url
attributes.description: Avatar image URL (foaf:img / vcard:hasPhoto). Falls back to initials.

declarations.summary: Account avatar + dropdown with WebID and sign-out.

declarations.description:
The suite's shared "report issue / give feedback / request help" control. Either
files the issue via an injected `submit` proxy (no GitHub account needed) or
opens GitHub's prefilled new-issue page. Presentation chrome — no RDF data model.
declarations.name: JeswrFeedbackButton

cssParts.description: The button that opens the feedback dialog.
cssParts.name: trigger

members.name: repo
members.description: REQUIRED: the OWNER/REPO the issue is filed against (each app passes its OWN).

members.name: appName
members.description: This app's human name, attached to diagnostics + used in the dialog copy.

members.name: appVersion
members.description: Optional build SHA / version, attached to diagnostics.

members.name: webId
members.description: The signed-in user's WebID. Attached ONLY if the consent box is ticked.

members.name: label
members.description: Trigger label (default "Feedback").

members.name: submit
members.description: Optional proxy hook — create the issue server-side.

members.name: tabbable
members.description:
The tabbable elements within `root`, collapsing each radio group to the
single member that participates in tab order (the checked radio, or the
first if none is checked) — mirroring the browser's real tab sequence so
Shift+Tab wrapping is correct after a non-default category is selected.

members.name: renderSuccessBody
members.description: The success body (shown once the proxy `submit` resolved): the issue link + Close.

members.name: renderCategoryChooser
members.description: The category radio group (one selectable card per FEEDBACK_CATEGORY).

members.name: renderForm
members.description: The feedback form (category + description + optional consent + actions).

events.description: Submitted; `detail` is the FeedbackPayload (fires for both mechanisms).
events.name: feedback-submit

attributes.name: repo
attributes.description: REQUIRED: the OWNER/REPO the issue is filed against (each app passes its OWN).

attributes.name: app-name
attributes.description: This app's human name, attached to diagnostics + used in the dialog copy.

attributes.name: app-version
attributes.description: Optional build SHA / version, attached to diagnostics.

attributes.name: webid
attributes.description: The signed-in user's WebID. Attached ONLY if the consent box is ticked.

declarations.summary: Feedback dialog that files a GitHub issue.

declarations.description:
A spinner with an optional label, announced via `role="status"` and respecting
`prefers-reduced-motion`. Presentation chrome — no RDF data model.
declarations.name: JeswrLoading

members.name: label
members.description: Optional text shown next to the spinner (also the accessible name).

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Optional text shown next to the spinner (also the accessible name).

declarations.description:
The suite's keystone login surface as a framework-agnostic Web Component. Wraps
the reactive-auth DPoP login flow + silent session restore behind the injected
LoginController seam. Presentation/auth chrome — no RDF data model (the WebID is
an identity, surfaced via the `.webId` accessor + the `session-change` event).
declarations.name: JeswrLoginPanel

cssParts.description: The "Use a different WebID" link button.
cssParts.name: add-account

slots.description: Content shown in the signed-in state (e.g. account links).

members.name: controller
members.description: The injected LoginController (the auth seam).

members.name: initialWebId
members.description: Optional pre-filled WebID for the input.

members.name: autoRestore
members.description: Attempt silent restore on connect (default true).

members.name: heading
members.description: Optional heading copy (default "Sign in").

members.name: #nativeFetch
members.description:
The fallback `.publicFetch` source until a controller is wired (after which
`.publicFetch` relays the controller's own pristine snapshot). Uses the
MODULE-LOAD snapshot (taken before any patching), NOT a construction-time re-read
of the global — so an unwired panel never exposes a patched/credentialed global
as the pristine fetch.

members.name: #restoreStarted
members.description: Single-flight guard so StrictMode / re-connect runs restore once.

members.name: #webIdEdited
members.description:
Whether the user has TYPED in the WebID input. While false, the input tracks the
`initial-webid` property (so changing `initial-webid` after connect updates the
field); once the user edits, their input is preserved.

members.name: #generation
members.description: Incremented on disconnect / new login so a stale async result is ignored.

members.name: fetch
members.description:
The AUTHENTICATED, session-bound fetch (after login). Before a session
exists — or with no controller — this is the pristine native fetch, so it is
always safe to call and never null. Use for the user's OWN origin(s).

members.name: publicFetch
members.description:
The PRISTINE native fetch (the foreign-origin / public-read boundary). NEVER
carries the session and never upgrades on a 401, so a session token cannot
leak cross-origin. Prefers the controller's own pre-patch snapshot; falls
back to the element's construction-time snapshot.

members.name: webId
members.description: The authenticated WebID, or null when logged out / no controller.

members.name: #reconcilePhase
members.description:
Sync `_phase` to the controller's CURRENT session: authenticated iff
`controller.webId`, else leave a restore-in-flight as-is, else the prompt. Cheap,
idempotent, no async — run on connect so the UI never shows a phase that
contradicts the controller (e.g. a stale "authenticated"/"authenticating" left by
an interrupted login when the controller actually has no session).

members.name: #maybeStartRestore
members.description:
Begin the on-load silent restore exactly once, when a controller is present
and auto-restore is on. If already logged in (controller has a webId), reflect
that immediately. Fail-closed: any restore error → the login prompt.

members.name: #doLogin
members.description: Run interactive login for a specific WebID (or undefined → re-login).

members.name: #emitSessionChange
members.description: Emit `session-change` reflecting the CURRENT controller session.

events.name: session-change
events.description: Session changed; `detail: { webId: string | null, loggedIn: boolean }`.

attributes.name: initial-webid
attributes.description: Optional pre-filled WebID for the input.

attributes.name: auto-restore
attributes.description: Attempt silent restore on connect (default true).

declarations.summary: Solid WebID login panel with silent session restore.

declarations.name: initialsOf
declarations.description: Avatar-fallback initials from a display name or a WebID. Exported for tests.

The small, non-intrusive "Saving… / Saved / Error" cue for the suite's
optimistic-mutation UX. A `state` attribute selects the rendering. Respects
`prefers-reduced-motion`. Presentation chrome — no RDF data model.
declarations.name: JeswrSavingIndicator

cssParts.description: The spinner shown in the "saving" state.
cssParts.name: spinner

members.name: state
members.description: The current mutation state. Reflected to the `state` attribute.

attributes.name: state
attributes.description: The current mutation state. Reflected to the `state` attribute.

declarations.description:
A framework-agnostic light/dark/system colour-theme switcher. Co-operative with
`@jeswr/app-shell`'s React ThemeProvider (same storage key + `.dark` class).
Presentation/behaviour chrome — no RDF data model.
declarations.name: JeswrThemeToggle

members.name: theme
members.description: The user's selected preference. Reflected to the `theme` attribute.

members.name: resolvedTheme
members.description: The concrete mode applied right now. Reflected to `resolved-theme`.

members.name: applyTheme
members.description:
Set the preference, resolve + apply it to <html>, (un)subscribe to the OS
media query, and (when `persist`) save it. Emits `theme-change`.

members.name: syncMediaListener
members.description: Subscribe to prefers-color-scheme only while in "system" mode.

attributes.name: theme
attributes.description: The user's selected preference. Reflected to the `theme` attribute.

attributes.name: resolved-theme
attributes.description: The concrete mode applied right now. Reflected to `resolved-theme`.
